purpose: Thin per-deposit INDEX into the recovered external metadata for this deposit's legacy Zenodo DOIs. The actual records live in the bulk data stores under data/ (openalex-severed-recovery.json, zenodo-xml-pretermination-2026-06-07.tar.gz, datacite-full-backup.json, datacite-survivors-multi-heteronym.json). Sidecars do not duplicate those records — they map each covered DOI to its identifier in each store.

10.5281/zenodo.18625272.title: Traversal Log: The Thousand Worlds Session — Google AI Mode as Generative Story Engine — Crimson Hexagon Archive

10.5281/zenodo.18627055.title: Traversal Log: The Consultant — Google AI Mode Generates an Enterprise Sales Pipeline from a Heteronym — Crimson Hexagon Archive

10.5281/zenodo.18633294.title: Grundrisse of Synthetic Coherence: Foundational Notes Toward a Political Economy of the Recursive Era — Crimson Hexagon Archive

10.5281/zenodo.18625242.title: Traversal Log: Documentation Rehearsal (TL;DR) — Google AI Mode Navigates the Crimson Hexagon via Paper Roses — Crimson Hexagon Archive

10.5281/zenodo.18626559.title: Traversal Log: The Recursive Self — Google AI Mode Reconstructs Psyche_OS from Two Search Terms — Crimson Hexagon Archive

openalex.lookup: records[].doi == this DOI; openalex_id is the W-prefixed OpenAlex Work ID

zenodo_bulk_xml.lookup: extract <record_id>.xml from the tarball where record_id is the digits after 'zenodo.'

datacite_survivor_sweep.lookup: search records[].attributes.doi == this DOI across orcid_records, name_records, sigil_records, vox_records, feist_records
